subterraCodes.title: Subterra Codes
subterraCodes.subtitle: Redeem working Subterra codes for Chrono Shards, TNT Blocks, EXP boosts, potions, and other progression rewards.
subterraCodes.intro: Use these codes at the Codes NPC in the surface lobby. Complete the tutorial first, then interact with the Codes NPC near the spawn gate, paste a code, and press Claim Code.

activeCodes.bestFor: Early Ability Card pulls and progression currency.
Mining runs, return safety, and ability-card progression.
Clearing terrain faster and leveling during focused play sessions.
Large Chrono Shard boost for cards and character growth.
Longer mining and combat sessions where EXP gain matters.
Starter progression after reaching the lobby.

redeemSteps: Complete the tutorial until you can freely move around the surface lobby.
Find the Codes NPC near the spawn gate.
Interact with the NPC, enter one working code, and press Claim Code.
Repeat the process for each active code.

subterraBeginnerGuide.title: Subterra Beginner Guide
subterraBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the basic loop: mine resources, manage inventory, upgrade your pickaxe and backpack, build with Ability Cards, craft gear, and survive deeper layers.
subterraBeginnerGuide.intro: Subterra is a 2.5D mining adventure where the world is procedurally generated and the layers become more hostile as you dig deeper. The clean starter route is simple: learn the controls, mine ores, upgrade tools, add Ability Cards, craft weapons or potions, then push into deeper layers while keeping each run profitable.

steps.title: Complete the Tutorial and Reach the Surface Lobby
steps.goal: Unlock free movement and learn where key NPCs and gates are located.
actions: Finish the opening tutorial.
Locate the spawn gate area.
Find the Codes NPC for reward redemption.
Use the lobby as your reset point before deeper mining runs.

steps.title: Learn the Core Controls
steps.goal: Move, mine, interact, block, and manage inventory without losing time underground.

steps.title: Mine Ores and Track Your Next Upgrade
steps.goal: Collect the materials needed for stronger pickaxes and larger backpacks.
actions: Use Left Mouse to mine blocks and ores.
Prioritize materials required by your next pickaxe or backpack tier.
Keep inventory space open for valuable ores.
Return to the surface when your bag is full or your run becomes risky.

steps.title: Upgrade Pickaxe First, Backpack Second
steps.goal: Improve mining power and speed before pushing too deep.
actions: Upgrade your pickaxe to mine faster and handle tougher layers.
Upgrade your backpack when short inventory runs start slowing progress.
Use early Gold, Rock, Coal, Copper, Tin, and Ingots for the first upgrade chain.

steps.title: Build Around Ability Cards
steps.goal: Use Ability Cards to shape your mining and survival style.
actions: Spend Chrono Shards on card progression.
Equip cards that improve your run efficiency.
Use code rewards to speed up early card access.

steps.title: Craft Weapons, Potions, and Recipes
steps.goal: Prepare for monsters, bosses, and longer underground runs.
actions: Craft weapons to fight enemies below the surface.
Look for rare weapon and potion recipes.
Use consumables before longer mining pushes.

steps.title: Explore Layers and Structures
steps.goal: Find deeper resources, lootable chests, cave structures, and progression materials.
actions: Move layer by layer instead of rushing straight down.
Open chests and inspect structures when safe.
Watch for stronger monsters as the depth increases.

steps.title: Fight Carefully and Use Block or Parry
steps.goal: Survive hostile layers while keeping mining runs profitable.
actions: Use F to block or parry incoming attacks.
Keep weapons ready when entering hostile sections.
Retreat when enemies or bosses make the run too expensive.

subterraPickaxeAndBackpackUpgrades.title: Subterra Pickaxe and Backpack Upgrades
subterraPickaxeAndBackpackUpgrades.subtitle: Plan early Subterra progression with pickaxe power, mining speed, backpack space, and upgrade recipes.
subterraPickaxeAndBackpackUpgrades.intro: Pickaxe upgrades raise your mining power and speed, while backpack upgrades increase inventory space. The first stretch is where materials are easiest to misallocate, so use the tier data below before spending.

pickaxeRows.priorityNote: Use this only long enough to gather Rock and Gold for Tier 2.
First practical upgrade for smoother surface mining.
Adds Coal to the early material route.
Start banking Raw Copper and Coal instead of spending them randomly.
Moves the route into Copper Ingot crafting.
A strong early jump in power; begin saving Tin materials.
Good checkpoint before heavier Tin and Copper costs.
Coal and Copper Ingot demand increases; avoid dumping them into side spending.
Citrine enters the route; keep gem drops for upgrades.
Begin collecting Raw Iron while maintaining Coal supply.
Material variety increases; track each ingredient before crafting side items.
Iron Ingot becomes central to the route.
Clean early-mid checkpoint before Silver-tier recipes start appearing.

backpackRows.priorityNote: Use until mining trips start ending because of inventory space.
First inventory upgrade for longer beginner mining loops.
Pairs well with early Copper and Tin progression.
Upgrade when Iron materials become common enough.
A midgame storage step that uses Silver and gem materials.
Save Amethyst and Emerald instead of burning them too early.
High-value gem materials start becoming important.
Platinum and Sapphire enter the storage path.
Diamond appears; do not spend rare gems blindly.
Large final crafted backpack checkpoint before gamepass storage.

priorities.title: Upgrade Pickaxe When Mining Feels Slow
priorities.description: Pickaxe tiers increase both power and mining speed, so they unlock faster routes through tougher blocks and deeper layers.

priorities.title: Upgrade Backpack When Trips End Too Early
priorities.description: Backpack upgrades are best when your inventory fills before your mining route is finished.

priorities.title: Save Gems for Required Recipes
priorities.description: Citrine, Topaz, Amethyst, Emerald, Ruby, Sapphire, and Diamond appear in upgrade recipes, so spending them early can delay later tiers.

priorities.title: Use Codes to Accelerate Card and EXP Progress
priorities.description: Chrono Shards, TNT Blocks, EXP boosts, and potions support the same mining loop that feeds pickaxe and backpack progression.

subterraWeaponsAndAbilityCardsTierList.title: Subterra Weapons and Ability Cards Tier List
subterraWeaponsAndAbilityCardsTierList.subtitle: Compare Subterra weapons and Ability Cards by damage, rarity, card limit, utility, and stage fit. This ranking is built for practical progression, mining efficiency, combat safety, and deeper-layer exploration.
subterraWeaponsAndAbilityCardsTierList.intro: Combat and mining builds depend on both weapon choice and Ability Card stacking. High-value setups usually combine one reliable weapon with cards that improve ore yield, pickaxe speed, backpack space, mobility, or survival in hostile layers.
subterraWeaponsAndAbilityCardsTierList.tierLegend: Tier letters rank overall progression value. Weapons are measured by damage and recipe tier; Ability Cards are measured by effect strength, card limit, and how broadly the effect improves a mining or combat route.

entries.name: Splitblade
entries.bestFor: Late-game melee damage and boss preparation
entries.utility: The strongest listed craftable greatsword option in the current weapon data, making it the main priority for players who want direct combat power.

entries.name: Lucky
entries.effect: 5% chance to double ore gained from mining an ore.
entries.bestFor: Ore farming, rare material farming, long mining routes
entries.utility: One of the best economy cards because it directly improves mining returns without changing route planning.

entries.name: Explosive Touch
entries.effect: 10% chance to create a small explosion when striking ores, damaging nearby blocks within a 2-block radius.
entries.bestFor: Dense ore pockets, geode clearing, faster tunnel expansion
entries.utility: Excellent for clearing clustered blocks and speeding up deeper mining sessions.

entries.name: Blazing Pickaxe
entries.effect: 30% chance to automatically smelt mined ores.
entries.bestFor: Ore routes that normally require repeated smeltery trips
entries.utility: Saves processing time and keeps mining routes more efficient.

recommendedCombos.name: Deep Ore Farmer
recommendedCombos.useCase: Maximizes ore yield, block clearing, and inventory efficiency during long mining runs.

recommendedCombos.name: Fast Smelter Build
recommendedCombos.useCase: Reduces time lost between mining, smelting, and selling.

entries.name: Knight's Greatsword
entries.utility: A strong upgrade path for players who need dependable melee damage before reaching the top greatsword option.

entries.name: Wretched Sword
entries.utility: Good damage for players progressing through tougher layers and enemy-dense routes.

entries.name: Bigger Backpack
entries.bestFor: Long mining sessions and sell-route efficiency
entries.utility: Reduces how often players must return to the surface to sell or manage inventory.

entries.name: Double Jump
entries.bestFor: Layer navigation, cave structures, escape routes
entries.utility: Improves movement in vertical caves, ruins, towers, and dangerous terrain.

entries.name: Larger Furnace
entries.utility: Improves the smelting loop when paired with backpack and ore-farming cards.

entries.name: Healthy
entries.bestFor: Surviving monsters, bosses, and explosion mistakes
entries.utility: Straightforward durability that becomes more valuable in deeper hostile layers.

recommendedCombos.name: Safe Deep Explorer
recommendedCombos.useCase: Balances survival, movement, and oxygen safety for deeper cave exploration.

recommendedCombos.name: Inventory Miner
recommendedCombos.useCase: Improves carry capacity and processing for ore-heavy routes.

entries.name: Sturdy Greatsword
entries.utility: A practical combat step before stronger greatswords become available.

entries.name: Reinforced Longbow
entries.utility: Useful for players who prefer distance against hostile monsters.

entries.name: Spine Bow
entries.utility: A solid bow option when players need range without committing to heavy melee positioning.

entries.name: Reinforced Bow
entries.utility: A stable ranged upgrade before stronger bows and longbows.

entries.name: Stronger Pickaxe
entries.bestFor: Mining tougher blocks and deeper ores
entries.utility: Improves block-breaking power and helps progression into harder layers.

entries.name: Lighter Pickaxe
entries.utility: A strong everyday mining card that improves the feel of nearly every route.

entries.name: Walk Speed
entries.utility: Simple movement speed that saves time across every mining loop.

entries.name: Extra Oxygen
entries.utility: Helps extend mining trips before returning to safer areas.

recommendedCombos.name: Early Miner
recommendedCombos.useCase: Improves mining speed, basic carry capacity, and movement while keeping combat usable.

recommendedCombos.name: Ranged Safety
recommendedCombos.useCase: Keeps distance from enemies while improving mobility and survival.

entries.name: Sword
entries.utility: A basic weapon option for players who need early defense while mining.

entries.name: Iron Greatsword
entries.utility: Useful as an early craftable step before stronger melee weapons.

entries.name: Curved Longbow
entries.utility: A basic longbow option for players who want range before stronger bows.

entries.name: Curved Bow
entries.utility: A low-commitment ranged option for early cave enemies.

entries.name: Magnet
entries.effect: Increases item pickup range by 0.5 studs.
entries.utility: Convenient for collecting drops and ores without tight positioning.

entries.name: Pouch
entries.utility: Helpful before Bigger Backpack becomes available.

entries.name: Radiant
entries.effect: Makes the player emit faint light.
entries.utility: Useful comfort card for navigation in dark layers.

entries.name: Sneaky
entries.utility: Good for players prioritizing mining routes over combat.

recommendedCombos.name: Starter Safety
recommendedCombos.useCase: Improves early inventory comfort, visibility, and basic survival.

recommendedCombos.name: Quiet Miner
recommendedCombos.useCase: Supports careful exploration while avoiding unnecessary monster pressure.

subterraCraftingRecipesAndMaterials.title: Subterra Crafting Recipes and Materials
subterraCraftingRecipesAndMaterials.subtitle: Track useful recipes, potion ingredients, explosive upgrades, keys, ore materials, ingots, crystals, and common resource sources. This table is built for quick filtering by crafting type, material source, and progression use.
subterraCraftingRecipesAndMaterials.intro: Crafting connects mining, monster drops, recipe scrolls, smelting, keys, potions, and explosives. Prioritize health recovery, chest access, sticky explosives, and ingot production because those systems directly affect exploration speed and deeper-layer survival.

items.progressionUse: Early survival, monster fights, deeper cave routes

items.name: Sticky C4

items.progressionUse: Late-game block clearing and deep-layer mining routes

subterraLayersOresAndStructures.title: Subterra Layers Ores and Structures
subterraLayersOresAndStructures.subtitle: Explore by layer, ore tier, structure type, portal access, and resource value. This module helps players decide where to mine, what to look for, and when to push deeper.
subterraLayersOresAndStructures.intro: The world becomes more dangerous as players descend through procedural cave layers. Each layer changes the block pool, ore value, structures, enemies, and exploration goals, so a strong route balances pickaxe upgrades, oxygen, weapons, keys, and inventory space.
subterraLayersOresAndStructures.layerLegend: Depth values reflect common progression breaks. Portal coordinates mark fast-travel unlocks; layers without a listed portal are reached by mining downward from the layer above.

layers.explorationFocus: Buy explosives and tools, smelt raw ores, sell loot, craft recipes, summon Ability Cards, and prepare for underground routes.
Farm early ores, collect rocks and coal, complete first quests, and prepare pickaxe upgrades before moving into Stone.
Build economy through ores and gems, open chests, watch for explosive enemies, and use keys for better loot.
Push into stronger ores, unlock fast travel, bring better weapons, and use mobility cards to handle vertical structures.
Farm higher-tier ores and crystals, manage tougher enemies, and prepare for Schwarzfrost progression.

layers.role: Current high-end exploration and boss layer
layers.explorationFocus: Target endgame materials, prepare for boss combat, and bring strong weapons, health recovery, mobility, and oxygen support.
Prioritize chests and villages for quests and loot, avoid TNT mistakes, use keys on higher-value chests, and use wormholes carefully for fast repositioning.

subterraMonstersBossesQuestsAndAchieveme.title: Subterra Monsters Bosses Quests and Achievements
subterraMonstersBossesQuestsAndAchieveme.subtitle: Track monsters, bosses, drops, quests, titles, and achievement rewards from early mining goals to deep-layer boss progression.
subterraMonstersBossesQuestsAndAchieveme.intro: Progression is not just mining downward. Monsters pressure cave routes, bosses guard deeper milestones, quests provide Gold and EXP, and achievements reward Chrono Shards for mining, chest hunting, exploration, and combat milestones.

cards.name: Zombie
cards.behavior: Chases the player and attacks at close range.
cards.counterplay: Keep space, use basic melee timing, and avoid fighting multiple enemies in narrow caves.

cards.name: Slime
cards.behavior: Chases the player and attacks at close range.
cards.counterplay: Useful early target for sticky explosive materials, but still dangerous when stacked with other enemies.

cards.name: Bomb Skeleton
cards.behavior: Runs at the player while holding a ticking bomb that explodes after a short delay.
cards.counterplay: Back away immediately, use terrain corners, and avoid panic-mining into dead ends.

cards.name: Darkstone Skeleton
cards.behavior: Chases the player through deeper layers and attacks at close range.
cards.counterplay: Bring stronger melee weapons, health cards, and mobility before farming Darkstone and Permafrost routes.

cards.name: Ice Elemental
cards.behavior: High-health frozen enemy found in advanced cold-layer routes.
cards.counterplay: Use stronger weapons, keep distance when possible, and avoid starting the fight without health recovery.

cards.name: Yrsa of the Sunken Seas
preparation: Use a high-damage weapon such as Splitblade or a strong greatsword.
Bring Healthy, Walk Speed, Double Jump, and Extra Oxygen for survival and positioning.
Carry health recovery before entering the boss route.
cards.counterplay: Treat the fight as a deep-layer combat check rather than a normal mining encounter.

cards.name: Ancient Lord Lorin
preparation: Prioritize late-game weapons, health cards, mobility cards, and recovery items.
cards.counterplay: Track boss drops separately from normal monster farming because artifact and recipe rewards belong in long-term progression.

cards.name: Daily Quests
requirements: Complete rotating mining or collection tasks such as gathering ores or breaking blocks.
cards.useCase: Best checked at the start of a play session so mining routes can match the active objectives.

cards.name: First Step
requirements: Upgrade your Pickaxe at the Blacksmith
cards.useCase: First structured progression goal for new players.

cards.name: Stone Champion
cards.useCase: Follow-up stone farming objective for early progression.

cards.name: Ore Hoarder
cards.useCase: Encourages players to diversify ore routes instead of farming only one material.

cards.name: Delver
cards.useCase: Good early economy quest while upgrading tools and basic materials.

cards.name: Shiny Hoarder
cards.useCase: Gem and ore checklist for deeper Stone Layer exploration.

cards.name: Deep Delver
cards.useCase: Mid-game mining quest for players staying longer underground.

cards.name: Diamond Seeker
cards.useCase: High-value gem objective for deeper mining routes.

cards.name: Amethyst Seeker
cards.useCase: Targets Amethyst Ore, Amethyst Blocks, and geode routes.

cards.name: Rocks Champion
cards.useCase: Simple material farming objective that pairs well with block-breaking achievements.

cards.name: Sapphire Seeker
cards.useCase: Gem route objective for players farming deeper valuable ores.

cards.name: Stalactite Striker
cards.objective: Impale an enemy with a Stalactite.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Subterra

questions.question: What is Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra is a 2.5D Roblox mining adventure by Polyworks Studio where players dig through hostile layers, upgrade pickaxes and backpacks, build with Ability Cards, craft weapons, and fight monsters underground.

questions.question: Where can I play Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra is available on Roblox. You can launch it directly from the official Roblox game page on PC, mobile, and supported consoles.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Subterra?
questions.answer: In Subterra, complete the tutorial, then interact with the Codes NPC near the spawn gate in the surface lobby, enter a working code, and press Claim Code to receive your rewards.

questions.question: What are Ability Cards in Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra Ability Cards are equippable modifiers bought with Chrono Shards. They improve mining, backpack space, movement, smelting, and combat, letting you shape your own mining and survival style.

questions.question: How do layers work in Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra layers are procedurally generated cave levels that become more hostile as you dig deeper. Each layer changes the block pool, ore value, structures, and enemies, so upgrade your pickaxe and gear before pushing down.

questions.question: Is Subterra free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Subterra is free to play on Roblox, with optional gamepass storage and cosmetic upgrades that do not block core mining progression.
